metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that adding the `code-review-*` solver arm to `eval_solver.run_case()` (feeding `case['diff']` as `{{ticket_context}}` and returning `{findings:[...]}`) is in scope for this story or for a prerequisite that must be merged first; AC-2's validation command is unexecutable without it.
findings.finding: `eval_solver.run_case()` has no execution arm for `code-review-*` prompt IDs — a call with `prompt_id='code-review-tests'` falls through to `raise ValueError(f"no eval solver for prompt {prompt_id!r}")`, so `rebar prompt eval code-review-tests` cannot execute and will error at runtime.
findings.location: AC line 2: 'validates offline (`rebar prompt eval code-review-tests` parses/loads)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add an `eval_solver.run_case` handler for `code-review-tests` (routing each case through the code-review overlay op), or explicitly scope AC2's 'validates offline' claim to structural YAML validation only and add a separate mechanism for behavioral eval of code-review overlays.
findings.finding: `eval_solver.run_case` has no handler for the `code-review-tests` prompt ID: it handles only `completion-verifier`, `ticket-quality`, `spec-alignment`, and plan-review criterion IDs. `code-review-tests` is a code-review overlay, not a plan-review criterion, so any dataset case in the eval spec would fall through to `raise ValueError(f'no eval solver for prompt {prompt_id!r}')`, meaning the recall and no-fire cases in AC2 cannot execute.
findings.location: AC2: 'code-review-tests.eval.yaml gains recall cases … and validates offline (`rebar prompt eval code-review-tests` parses/loads)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm (or add) a `code-review-*` execution arm in `eval_solver.run_case()` before claiming `rebar prompt eval code-review-tests` parses/loads; the current solver raises `ValueError` for any `code-review-*` prompt_id.
findings.finding: eval_solver.run_case() has NO execution arm for code-review prompt ids: reading eval_solver.py lines 106–171 shows it handles only completion-verifier, ticket-quality, spec-alignment, and plan-review criteria, then raises ValueError for anything else. The plan's AC3 states `rebar prompt eval code-review-tests` 'parses/loads', but that CLI command routes through run_case, which would raise ValueError for prompt_id='code-review-tests'. The f93a ticket (the stated prerequisite) is closed but the solver arm is absent from the current codebase.
findings.location: AC: `code-review-tests.eval.yaml` gains recall cases … validates offline (`rebar prompt eval code-review-tests` parses/loads)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a story task (or expand this story's scope) to add an arm in eval_solver.run_case that, for prompt_id values matching the code-review overlay catalog, writes a fixture diff as `ticket_context`, runs the overlay prompt via the CodeReviewBatchRunner, and returns `{findings:[...]}` — matching the pattern described in f93a's implementation comment.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py` to the Files section (or explicitly note it as a prerequisite from f93a), explaining how `run_case('code-review-tests', ...)` will execute: the diff-based input path and the `{findings:[...]}` output shape.
findings.finding: `eval_solver.py` has no execution arm for `code-review-*` prompt ids, yet AC2 requires the eval to 'validate offline' via `rebar prompt eval code-review-tests`. The plan does not enumerate `eval_solver.py` as a file requiring changes, but `run_case()` currently raises `ValueError` for any prompt_id that is not a plan-review criterion, completion-verifier, ticket-quality, or spec-alignment.
findings.location: Files section (omits eval_solver.py) / AC2: 'validates offline (rebar prompt eval code-review-tests)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether f93a's code-review eval solver arm (`run_case` branch for overlay prompt ids) is present and working; if absent, add it (or explicitly scope it to this story) so the recall/no-fire eval cases in `code-review-tests.eval.yaml` can actually execute and enforce AC2's behavioral guarantee.
findings.finding: The plan's AC ('the overlay does not over-suppress valid patterns nor over-fire on them') requires the eval harness to actually RUN recall/no-fire cases through the code-review-tests overlay, but eval_solver.run_case() has no code-review overlay execution arm in the current codebase — and the plan neither confirms f93a's solver arm landed nor scopes adding it here.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria — AC2 ('rebar prompt eval code-review-tests parses/loads; the overlay does not over-suppress valid patterns nor over-fire on them')
findings.suggested_fix: In eval_solver.run_case(), add a code-review overlay arm: detect prompt_id starting with 'code-review-' (excluding 'code-review-verify'), treat case['diff'] (fallback case['input']) as the {{ticket_context}} payload, run the overlay prompt in structured mode, and return its native {findings:[...]} output. This mirrors the design f93a's comment described.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `code-review-tests.eval.yaml` truly pre-exists from f93a or must be created as part of this story; if the latter, remove the 'already exists' qualifier and add file creation to the scope.
findings.finding: The plan states `code-review-tests.eval.yaml` 'already exists from f93a', but the file is absent from `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/` and from `.rebar/evals/`. Running `rebar prompt eval code-review-tests` will fail with `EvalError: no eval spec for prompt 'code-review-tests'` until the file is created, making AC2's offline-validation claim unachievable as stated.
findings.location: Files section / AC2: 'code-review-tests.eval.yaml (already exists from f93a; ADD recall cases…)'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the Files entry to: `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml (CREATE — recall cases for change-detector / over-mocking / source-grepping / existence-only, and no-fire cases for each FP-guard family)`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one eval recall case each for the 'tautological' (with inverted-trace disproof) and 'under-mocking' recall patterns to `code-review-tests.eval.yaml`, or explicitly justify their omission in the AC.
findings.finding: Two of the five defined recall patterns — 'tautological' (with inverted-trace disproof) and 'under-mocking' — are specified in the overlay content (What section) but have NO corresponding eval recall case listed in the Files section or AC. This means the eval suite will not enforce that the overlay correctly fires on these antipatterns, leaving their recall unvalidated.
findings.location: Files section (code-review-tests.eval.yaml bullet) and AC line 2 vs. What section recall patterns
findings.suggested_fix: In the Files section bullet for code-review-tests.eval.yaml, extend the recall case list to: 'change-detector / tautological / over-mocking / under-mocking / source-grepping / existence-only'. For tautological, provide a snippet where a mock's configured return value is asserted directly (and a paired no-fire case where the inverted-trace disproof shows it IS catching real behavior). For under-mocking, provide a snippet where a DB/network call is not mocked in a unit test.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify (or correct) whether `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml` exists on the branch being merged; if f93a did not land it, scope AC-2 as CREATE (not ADD) and confirm the file will be authored as part of this story.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `code-review-tests.eval.yaml` 'already exists from f93a', but the file is absent from `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/` — the directory listing shows no `code-review-tests.eval.yaml` entry.
findings.location: Files section / AC line 2: 'code-review-tests.eval.yaml (already exists from f93a; ADD recall cases…)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that `code-review-tests.eval.yaml` must be CREATED (it does not exist); update the Files section to reflect creation, and ensure it includes all required top-level YAML keys (`prompt`, `model`, `epochs`, `gate`, `scorers`, `dataset`).
findings.finding: `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml` does not exist in the repo — the plan's claim 'already exists from f93a' is incorrect. The eval_specs directory contains no code-review-*.eval.yaml files, and f93a's own plan-review manifest records 'dep absent src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/' at signing time.
findings.location: Files section / AC2: `code-review-tests.eval.yaml (already exists from f93a; ADD recall cases …)`
findings.suggested_fix: Change the AC2 parenthetical from '(already exists from f93a; ADD recall cases …)' to '(CREATE this file; seed with recall cases … and no-fire cases …)' to match the actual repo state.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen AC2's proving command to require that `rebar prompt eval code-review-tests` **passes** (not merely parses/loads) — i.e., the eval runner reports correct verdicts for the new recall cases (expected: finding flagged) AND the new no-fire cases (expected: no finding raised) — so the behavioral end-state is actually exercised, not just the YAML structure.
findings.finding: The proving command for AC2's behavioral claim ('the overlay does not over-suppress valid patterns nor over-fire on them') is specified only as 'parses/loads' — a structural YAML validation — which does not exercise the overlay's actual LLM judgment on the new eval cases. A YAML file can parse/load successfully while every new eval case produces wrong results, leaving the behavioral end-state unverified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'code-review-tests.eval.yaml gains recall cases AND no-fire cases... validates offline (`rebar prompt eval code-review-tests` parses/loads); the overlay does not over-suppress valid patterns nor over-fire on them.'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the parenthetical in AC2 with: 'validates offline (`rebar prompt eval code-review-tests` **passes**, with all new recall cases producing a flagged-finding verdict and all new no-fire cases producing a no-finding verdict)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the overlay-authoring + eval pattern (overlay carries domain rubric, verifier stays domain-blind, eval enforces recall + no-fire) in an ADR, CLAUDE.md section, or design doc before or alongside this first implementation so all six da8a children share a documented contract.
findings.finding: The plan explicitly frames this story as proving a novel 'overlay-authoring + eval pattern' that will be replicated for five more specialists (da8a children), but no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc entry is planned to capture this architectural decision — leaving the pattern undocumented before it is industrialized across the remaining overlays.
findings.location: Plan body — 'DO THIS FIRST to prove the overlay-authoring + eval pattern before the other five specialists (all six are children of da8a)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sub-task: 'Update CLAUDE.md (or create docs/adr/overlay-pattern.md) with: (1) the overlay/verifier separation principle, (2) the required eval structure (recall cases + no-fire cases per FP-guard family), (3) the naming convention for overlay files and eval specs.' Reference this doc from each of the six overlay stories.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the `code_review_*` gating scorers promised by f93a's AC are present in `eval_scorers.REGISTRY`; if absent, add them (or confirm that existing generic names like `recall_on_seeded_defects`/`no_fire_on_good_cases` are intentionally reused) before authoring the eval spec.
findings.finding: `eval_scorers.REGISTRY` contains no `code_review_*` scorer names — any eval spec for `code-review-tests` that references a code-review-specific scorer will fail `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` with 'scorer name not registered', so the spec cannot be validated offline.
findings.location: AC line 2: 'validates offline (`rebar prompt eval code-review-tests` parses/loads)'
findings.suggested_fix: If reusing generic scorer names is the intent, note it explicitly in the plan; if dedicated names are needed, add e.g. `'recall_on_test_antipatterns': _recall` and `'no_fire_on_valid_test_patterns': _no_fire` to REGISTRY in eval_scorers.py.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either enumerate `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py` as an edit target (adding `code_review_*` scorer aliases) or explicitly state that the existing `recall_on_seeded_defects` / `no_fire_on_good_cases` names will be reused and explain why they are sufficient for the code-review-tests cases.
findings.finding: `eval_scorers.py` REGISTRY contains no `code_review_*` scorer names, yet AC2's eval cases need deterministic scorer names that gate recall and no-fire assertions. The plan does not enumerate `eval_scorers.py` as a file requiring changes.
findings.location: Files section (omits eval_scorers.py) / AC2: recall cases and no-fire cases that 'validate offline'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the plan's 'already exists from f93a' premise: `code-review-tests.eval.yaml` is absent; the story must CREATE it (not just add cases), and the plan should specify or reference the required YAML skeleton (prompt, model, epochs, gate, coverage_threshold, scorers).
findings.finding: The plan instructs implementors to ADD cases to `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml` ('already exists from f93a'), but the file is absent from the repo. The eval_specs directory listing contains no `code-review-tests.eval.yaml` (or any `code-review-*.eval.yaml`), and a filename search confirms zero matches. The plan's framing as an ADD-to-existing operation is incorrect; the file must be created from scratch, and the plan provides no starter structure (prompt id, model, epochs, gate, coverage_threshold, scorers, dataset schema).
findings.location: Files section: `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml` (already exists from f93a; ADD recall cases …)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the eval dataset case payload field name for code-review overlay cases (e.g. `diff:` or `input:`) so implementors know what field the solver arm reads as `{{ticket_context}}`; confirm `validate_dataset_and_gold` recognises this key as a valid payload.
findings.finding: The plan specifies eval dataset cases for the new eval spec (recall antipatterns + no-fire FP-guard cases) but does not define the case payload field name that the code-review solver arm would use as the `{{ticket_context}}` diff input. Other eval specs use `input`, `ticket_context`, or `spec`/`epics` depending on the reviewer type. The f93a implementation comment specifies `case['diff']` (fallback `case['input']`), but this convention is documented nowhere in the plan or in the current codebase, leaving the case schema unspecified for the implementor authoring the eval YAML.
findings.location: Files section: `code-review-tests.eval.yaml` — 'ADD recall cases for change-detector / over-mocking / source-grepping / existence-only, and no-fire cases …'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note to the plan (or to the eval spec skeleton) that each code-review eval case must supply a `diff:` (or `input:`) field containing a minimal unified diff illustrating the antipattern or the valid pattern; update validate_dataset_and_gold's metadata_keys exclusion list to include `diff` if that is the chosen field name.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
